headline: Speaker's ruling on 12 controversial motions cannot be challenged
context: The Dewan Rakyat Speaker made 12 contested rulings in 2025 on admissibility of opposition motions, with no mechanism for appeal or review of Speaker's decisions.

cards.big: Speaker blocked 12 opposition motions — with no appeal mechanism available
cards.sub: Single individual's discretion determines what Parliament can and cannot debate

cards.big: Standing Order 43 gives Speaker absolute discretion over motion admissibility
cards.sub: No provision exists for challenging or reviewing Speaker's procedural decisions

cards.big: Blocked motions included debate on RM2.3 billion flood fund mismanagement
cards.sub: Significant public interest topics suppressed through procedural gate-keeping

cards.big: UK Parliament allows debate on Speaker's rulings through substantive motion process
cards.sub: Westminster model includes safeguards against unchecked Speaker authority

cards.big: When the Speaker answers to the government, parliamentary debate answers to the government too
Should a parliamentary procedures committee have power to review contested Speaker rulings?
